ErrorCode.description:
Standardized error codes for Stargate API (Contract v1.0)

These codes allow consumers to programmatically handle errors
and determine appropriate retry strategies.
ErrorCode.title: ErrorCode

ErrorResponse.description:
Standard error response format (Contract v1.0)

All Stargate errors return HTTP 200 with status='error' to enable
structured error handling by AI agents. The error_code field allows
programmatic error classification and retry strategy selection.

capability_key.description: The capability that was being executed (if applicable)
capability_key.title: Capability Key

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling

retry_strategy.description: Recommended retry strategy for this error type

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse

RetryStrategy.description:
Recommended retry strategy for each error type

Consumers should follow these strategies when handling errors.
RetryStrategy.title: RetryStrategy

turn_id.description: Turn ID for idempotency (prevents duplicate executions) - REQUIRED

use_delegation.description: For agent credentials: prefer delegated access over system credentials

user_id.description: User ID for credential lookup and audit

get.description: Root endpoint - health check
List all available capabilities

schema.title: Response Get Capabilities Api V1 Capabilities Get

get.description:
List capabilities with schema availability indicator.

Returns all capabilities with a 'schema_available' field indicating
whether rich metadata is available for each capability.

This is useful for AI agents to know which capabilities have detailed
documentation vs. basic registry entries.

schema.title: Response List Capabilities Enhanced Api V1 Capabilities Enhanced Get

post.description:
Check connector authentication status for specific services (workflow context).

This endpoint is called by the workflow manifest builder to check which
connectors are authenticated before presenting a workflow preview to the user.

get.description:
Get metadata about a stored credential (without exposing tokens)

Query params:
- org_id: Organization ID
- user_id: User ID
- service: Service name (e.g., "quickbooks", "google")
- credential_type: "agent" or "customer" (default: "customer")

Returns credential metadata: expiry, realm_id, etc. without exposing access_token

schema.title: Response Get Credential Metadata Api V1 Credentials Metadata Get

post.description:
Revoke/delete a stored credential (disconnect an integration).

Request body:
{
    "org_id": "org_123",
    "user_id": "user_456",
    "service": "quickbooks",
    "credential_type": "customer"  // optional, defaults to "customer"
}

Returns:
- status: "revoked" or "not_found"
- service: service name

schema.title: Response Revoke Credential Api V1 Credentials Revoke Post

post.description:
Check if credentials exist for a capability before execution

Request body:
{
    "org_id": "org_123",
    "user_id": "user_456",
    "capability_key": "vendor.create",
    "use_delegation": false
}

Returns:
- credential_available: bool
- credential_type: "agent" | "customer" | None
- access_pattern: "programmatic" | "delegate" | None (for agent credentials)
- requires_setup: bool (True if credential missing)
- delegation_supported: bool (True if this capability supports delegation)
- delegation_instructions: str (setup instructions if delegation supported)

schema.title: Response Check Credential Status Api V1 Credentials Status Post

post.description:
Execute a tool based on capability_key.

This is the MAIN endpoint that the Brain (MARS) calls.
Supports idempotency via turn_id with 24-hour cache.
Session ID can be passed via X-Session-ID header or session_id body field.

**Response Format (Contract v1.0):**
- Success: `{"status": "success", "capability_key": "...", "outputs": {...}}`
- Error: `{"status": "error", "error_code": "...", "error_message": "..."}`

Rate limited: 100 requests per minute per org_id (configurable via env vars).

schema.title: Response 200 Execute Tool Api V1 Execute Post

200.description: Successful execution or error (Stargate always returns 200)

schema.title: Response List Capability Schemas Api V1 Schemas Get

get.description:
Get detailed schema for a specific capability.

Returns rich metadata including parameters, returns, errors, and workflow hints.
The capability_key should match the key used in /api/v1/execute.

Combines schema metadata with registry metadata for complete information.

schema.title: Response Get Capability Schema Api V1 Schemas  Capability Key  Get
Response List Services With Schemas Api V1 Services Get

get.description: Health check endpoint with real DB and Redis pings.

Detailed health check for all connectors
Shows credential status, expiry, and connection counts per service

Initiate Airtable OAuth flow with PKCE.

Airtable requires PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for security.
The code_verifier is embedded in the signed state parameter, making
this flow completely stateless and compatible with multi-worker deployments.

Args:
    org_id: Organization identifier
    user_id: User identifier
    credential_type: Type of credential (customer/agent)

Returns:
    Redirect to Airtable authorization page
Handle Airtable OAuth callback with PKCE verification. Redirects to N3 on completion.

Redirects user to Asana authorization page

get.description:
Initiate DocuSign OAuth flow.

Redirects user to DocuSign authorization page to grant access to
e-signature capabilities including sending, signing, and managing envelopes.

Args:
    org_id: Organization identifier
    user_id: User identifier
    credential_type: Type of credential (customer/agent)

Returns:
    Redirect to DocuSign authorization page
Handle DocuSign OAuth callback. Redirects to N3 on completion.

Initiate Google Workspace OAuth flow

Redirects user to Google authorization page

Args:
    service: gmail, drive, calendar, sheets, all (determines scopes)
    credential_type: "customer" or "agent"

Handle Google OAuth callback

Exchange authorization code for access/refresh tokens and store them.
Redirects to N3 frontend on completion.

Initiate Gusto OAuth flow.

Redirects user to Gusto authorization page to grant access to payroll data.

Args:
    org_id: Organization identifier
    user_id: User identifier
    credential_type: Type of credential (customer/agent)

Returns:
    Redirect to Gusto authorization page

Handle Gusto OAuth callback.

Exchange authorization code for access/refresh tokens and store them.
Redirects to N3 frontend on completion.

Initiate HubSpot OAuth flow

Redirects user to HubSpot authorization page

Handle HubSpot OAuth callback

Exchange authorization code for access/refresh tokens and store them.
Redirects to N3 frontend on completion.

Initiate Linear OAuth flow with agent mode (actor=app)

Creates a dedicated agent user in Linear workspace
Agent can be assigned issues and @mentioned

Handle Linear OAuth callback

Exchange authorization code for access/refresh tokens
Linear tokens expire after 24 hours, refresh tokens auto-enabled (Oct 2025+)

schema.title: Response Linear Oauth Callback Oauth Linear Callback Get

get.description:
Initiate Shopify OAuth flow.

Shopify requires the shop name to construct OAuth URLs.
The shop parameter should be the myshopify.com subdomain (e.g., 'my-store').

Args:
    org_id: Organization identifier
    user_id: User identifier
    shop: Shopify store name (e.g., 'my-store' for my-store.myshopify.com)
    credential_type: Type of credential (customer/agent)

Returns:
    Redirect to Shopify authorization page
Handle Shopify OAuth callback with HMAC validation. Redirects to N3.

Initiate Slack OAuth flow

Redirects user to Slack authorization page
Note: Slack is always an agent credential (bot acting on behalf of workspace)

Handle Slack OAuth callback

Exchange authorization code for access token and store credentials.
Redirects to N3 frontend on completion.

Redirects user to Square authorization page to grant access to
payments, orders, customers, and inventory data.

Args:

    user_id: User identifier
    credential_type: Type of credential (customer/agent)

Returns:
    Redirect to Square authorization page
Handle Square OAuth callback. Redirects to N3 on completion.

Initiate Stripe Connect OAuth flow

Redirects user to Stripe authorization page

Handle Stripe Connect OAuth callback

Exchange authorization code for access token and store credentials.
Redirects to N3 frontend on completion.

Initiate Xero OAuth flow

Redirects user to Xero authorization page with accounting scopes.

Handle Xero OAuth callback

Exchange authorization code for tokens, fetch tenant ID from
/connections, and store credentials.
